source_title: Natural Language Processing (NLP) Tutorial with Python & NLTK

concepts.name: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
concepts.definition: A part of computer science and artificial intelligence that deals with human language to organize text data, automate tasks, and solve problems.

concepts.name: Natural Language Understanding (NLU)
concepts.definition: The process of mapping natural language input into useful representations and analyzing different aspects of the language.

concepts.name: Natural Language Generation (NLG)
concepts.definition: The process of producing meaningful phrases and sentences in natural language from internal representations.

concepts.name: Lexical Ambiguity
concepts.definition: The presence of two or more possible meanings within a single word.

concepts.name: Syntactical Ambiguity
concepts.definition: The presence of two or more possible meanings within a single sentence or sequence of words.

concepts.name: Referential Ambiguity
concepts.definition: Ambiguity arising from unclear references in a sentence.

steps.explanation: To ensure accuracy and correctness in written communication.
steps.gotcha: Contextual errors may not be detected.

steps.explanation: To enable efficient search and retrieval of information.
steps.gotcha: Synonyms and polysemous words can complicate results.

steps.explanation: To understand public opinion or customer feedback.
steps.gotcha: Sarcasm and irony can lead to misinterpretation.

steps.action: Translate text from one language to another
steps.gotcha: Cultural nuances may not be accurately translated.

gotchas.context: When a word has multiple meanings in a sentence.
gotchas.solution: Use context clues or disambiguation techniques.

gotchas.context: When a sentence can be parsed in multiple ways.
gotchas.solution: Ensure grammatically correct parsing using NLP tools.

gotchas.description: Misinterpretation due to sarcasm or irony
gotchas.solution: Use advanced models trained on contextually rich datasets.
